purpose: Track whether STAX reduces Codex cleanup burden and catches real project-control proof failures.

tasks.task: Audit ADMISSION-APP publish/sync readiness and produce one bounded, non-publishing proof step.
tasks.staxInitialMiss: Initial project_control answer drifted to iOS/TestFlight preflight because injected repo evidence contained release docs, even though the user task was publish/sync readiness.
patchesMade: Prioritize explicit ADMISSION-APP publish/sync task intent over unrelated iOS release evidence.
Classify pwsh missing during preflight as clean failure, not fake-complete.

codexAction.firstRemainingFailure: pwsh is not installed; cannot run tools/validate-sync-surface.ps1 in this environment.

tasks.notes: This task produced useful dogfood signal: STAX was safe but initially mis-targeted the proof lane; after patching it correctly kept publish/sync blocked and labeled the failed preflight as clean failure.

tasks.task: Audit Sports Wellness layout readiness and require rendered proof for text fit, border symmetry, and SMART goals/checkmark containment.
tasks.staxInitialMiss: Initial project_control answer included a stray Brightspace verified line because repo evidence from earlier benchmark context leaked into a canvas-helper bounded-prompt task.
patchesMade: Keep canvas-helper visual-proof project_control prompts from leaking Brightspace context.
Added a regression test for canvas-helper Sports Wellness visual proof prompts.
tasks.notes: STAX correctly required rendered proof instead of accepting CSS/source claims. In-app browser screenshots showed the SMART goals section text fitting at mobile width and the completion control contained after marking Phase 1 complete. No canvas-helper source files were changed.

tasks.task: Audit current STAX uncommitted campaign/dogfood/comparison-integrity changes before any commit-ready claim.
tasks.staxInitialMiss: The first project_control self-audit leaked ADMISSION-APP/TestFlight/SHEETS context from prior evidence and returned the wrong repo lane.
patchesMade: Added an explicit STAX commit-readiness/dogfood task lane.
Scoped the STAX lane narrowly so normal STAX memory/proof benchmark cases still use their existing proof rules.
Added a regression test preventing ADMISSION-APP/TestFlight/Sports Wellness leakage in STAX commit-readiness audits.

commandsRun: npm run rax -- run "Extract this as STAX fitness signals: Dean trained jiu jitsu Saturday for 90 minutes."

tasks.notes: This task showed the full dogfood loop working: STAX exposed a wrong-context miss, a focused regression caught the issue, and the full required validation passed afterward.

tasks.task: Audit whether scraped admissions data contains the right fields and coverage for the app it built.
tasks.staxInitialMiss: The first project_control answer routed scrape/data correctness to Sheets publish/sync safety instead of a data-contract and field-coverage audit.
patchesMade: Added a scrape/data correctness project_control lane for ADMISSION-APP.
Prevented scrape/data correctness tasks from being swallowed by Sheets publish/sync safety.
Added a regression test for data-contract audit routing.

codexAction.firstRemainingFailure: Data shape validates and fixture checks pass, but coverage for app-consumed admissions requirement fields is too sparse to call scrape/data correctness proven.

tasks.notes: The safe conclusion is that ADMISSION-APP has valid canonical shape and passing fixture checks, but scrape/data quality is not proven good enough for app correctness because many core requirement fields are blank.

tasks.task: Audit the Codex report from the ADMISSION-APP scrape/data coverage audit.
tasks.staxInitialMiss: STAX stayed safe but repeated the same data-contract audit even though the Codex report already supplied coverage results and fixture outcomes.
patchesMade: Detected supplied scrape/data coverage audit results in project_control.
Moved next action from rerunning the same audit to tracing the first concrete coverage gap.
Added a regression test for supplied ADMISSION-APP coverage audits.
tasks.notes: After patching, STAX correctly treated the supplied audit as provisional but advanced to the first concrete data gap: why Avg_Total and core requirement fields are blank for most canonical rows.

tasks.task: Trace the first concrete ADMISSION-APP Avg_Total coverage gap without mutating canonical data.

codexAction.firstRemainingFailure: Avg_Total is blank mostly because extraction/candidate generation has not covered most canonical rows; the Water and Wastewater example also shows canonical/index URL and credential drift.

tasks.notes: This task produced a useful concrete blocker: do not patch app logic yet. The next ADMISSION move should be a bounded candidate-generation/identity-alignment proof for one institution/field, not publish/sync.

tasks.task: Audit the Avg_Total gap trace report and choose the next bounded action.
tasks.staxInitialMiss: STAX misread safety text saying no publish/sync was run as a Sheets publish/sync task, instead of staying on Avg_Total coverage and identity drift.
patchesMade: Expanded scrape/data correctness intent detection to include Avg_Total coverage, identity drift, and canonical data gap language.
Expanded supplied coverage detection to recognize row-count/Avg_Total gap reports and one-row candidate artifacts.
Added a regression test preventing Avg_Total gap reports from routing to Sheets publish safety.
tasks.notes: After patching, STAX kept the task in the data-gap lane and recommended tracing why Avg_Total/core requirements are blank, instead of re-entering Sheets publish/sync safety.

tasks.task: Audit Brightspace dependency/build/ingest readiness and choose one bounded next proof step.
tasks.staxInitialMiss: The first project_control answer leaked ADMISSION-APP/iOS/TestFlight context into an explicit Brightspace dependency readiness task.
patchesMade: Added explicit Brightspace project-control isolation so dependency prompts do not inherit ADMISSION release or app-store context.
Added a Brightspace default proof lane that asks for dependency inspection before build/ingest claims.
Added a regression test preventing Brightspace dependency prompts from leaking ADMISSION release context.

codexAction.dependencyInspectionSummary: vite@7.3.1, rollup@4.59.0, and @rollup/rollup-darwin-arm64@4.59.0 are installed in the Brightspace dependency tree.
codexAction.firstRemainingFailure: Dependency presence is proven, but build and ingest are not proven until their commands run.

tasks.notes: After patching, STAX kept the task on Brightspace and correctly chose dependency inspection as the next bounded proof step instead of claiming build/ingest readiness.

tasks.task: Audit the Brightspace dependency proof and decide whether build/ingest readiness is proven.

codexAction.ingestCiSummary: ingest:ci ran build, promotion-check, intake manifest validation for 35 manifests, the focused ingest suite, and 22 manual ingest benchmarks successfully.

tasks.notes: This completed the Brightspace proof chain: dependency inspection, build, and ingest:ci all passed, with no new tracked Brightspace changes from the proof commands.

tasks.task: Audit the STAX dogfood campaign state before calling it complete.
tasks.staxInitialMiss: The first self-audit was run as a one-line shell argument, so the labeled Command Evidence block was not parsed; before patching, the STAX commit-readiness lane also did not have a specific dogfood campaign completion path for supplied validation evidence.
patchesMade: Added dogfood campaign audit detection for STAX project_control tasks.
Added supplied STAX validation evidence recognition for typecheck, tests, and eval.
Added a regression test proving supplied validation evidence is accepted while a 9/10 campaign remains incomplete.

commandsRun: npm run rax -- run "Extract this as STAX fitness signals: Dean trained jiu jitsu Saturday for 90 minutes."

tasks.notes: This closes the 10-task dogfood loop without overclaiming: STAX now accepts supplied local validation evidence, still rejects 9/10 as incomplete, and the final recorded action is to update the campaign ledger/report to 10/10.
